item.title: Som Distilleries & Breweries Ltd Declines 1.51% Amid 52-Week Low and Market Volatility
item.description: Som Distilleries & Breweries Ltd experienced a challenging week ending 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 1.51% to close at Rs.79.15, despite the broader Sensex falling 3.00%. The stock hit a fresh 52-week low early in the week, reflecting ongoing financial pressures and bearish technical signals amid volatile market conditions. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Jaiprakash Associates Ltd Falls 15.28%: 6 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: Jaiprakash Associates Ltd endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price plunging 15.28% to close at Rs.2.44, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% decline over the same period. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows and lower circuit limits amid heavy selling pressure, deteriorating fundamentals, and weak technical signals, reflecting sustained bearish momentum and investor aversion. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Vascon Engineers Ltd Falls 8.42%: Valuation Upgrade Amidst Market Challenges
item.description: Vascon Engineers Ltd experienced a challenging week on the bourses, with its share price declining by 8.42% from Rs.38.00 to Rs.34.80, significantly underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. Despite this price weakness, the company’s valuation metrics improved notably, prompting MarketsMOJO to upgrade its rating from 'Strong Sell' to 'Sell' on 4 March 2026. The week was marked by a mix of valuation appeal and ongoing financial headwinds, reflecting a cautious market sentiment towards the stock. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: ITI Ltd Falls 4.05% Amid 52-Week Low and Elevated Volatility: Key Weekly Insights
item.description: ITI Ltd’s stock declined 4.05% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.259.15, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The telecom equipment company faced a turbulent week marked by a new 52-week low, significant gap down openings, and persistent bearish technical signals. Despite some intraday resilience relative to its sector, the stock’s overall trend remained negative amid weak financial metrics and elevated volatility. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: True Green Bio Energy Ltd Surges 51.83% in a Week: 7 Key Developments Driving the Rally
item.description: True Green Bio Energy Ltd delivered a remarkable weekly gain of 51.83%, closing at Rs.137.74 on 6 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock’s strong momentum was fuelled by a series of technical breakthroughs, valuation shifts, and record price milestones, marking a week of intense market activity and investor interest. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Titagarh Rail Systems Ltd Falls 3.08%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Titagarh Rail Systems Ltd’s stock closed the week at Rs.679.55, down 3.08% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.701.15, marginally underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp declines early on, hitting fresh 52-week lows amid ongoing earnings pressure, followed by a modest recovery in the final two trading sessions. Despite the late-week gains, the stock remains under significant pressure from valuation concerns and subdued profitability metrics. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Voltas Ltd. Declines 5.47%: 3 Key Technical Shifts and Market Dynamics This Week
item.description: Voltas Ltd. experienced a challenging week, closing at Rs.1,478.35 on 6 March 2026, down 5.47% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.1,563.85. This decline outpaced the broader Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period, reflecting mixed technical momentum and heightened market volatility. The week was marked by significant shifts in derivatives activity, technical indicators, and price fluctuations that collectively shaped investor sentiment. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Hindustan Oil Exploration Gains 19.84%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Surge
item.description: Hindustan Oil Exploration Company Ltd (HOEC) delivered a remarkable weekly performance, surging 19.84% from Rs.130.80 to Rs.156.75 between 2 and 6 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. This strong rally was driven by a series of positive intraday price movements and mixed but improving technical signals amid a volatile market backdrop. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Supreme Holdings & Hospitality Ltd Falls 7.94%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Supreme Holdings & Hospitality Ltd’s stock declined sharply by 7.94% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows amid deteriorating financials, a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, and persistent bearish technical signals, reflecting sustained challenges in profitability and market sentiment. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Saven Technologies Ltd Falls 1.70%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Saven Technologies Ltd’s stock closed the week at Rs.36.47, down 1.70% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.37.10, while the Sensex declined 3.00% over the same period. Despite the modest weekly loss, the stock outperformed the broader market index, reflecting mixed investor sentiment amid valuation upgrades and a fresh 52-week low earlier in the week. Time: 57 minutes ago

item.title: Welspun Living Ltd Falls 3.99%: 2 Key Technical Signals Drive the Week
item.description: Welspun Living Ltd experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining 3.99% to close at Rs.120.35, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant technical developments, including a shift to a bearish momentum on 4 March and the formation of a Death Cross on 6 March, signalling potential sustained weakness ahead. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Persistent Systems Gains 0.89%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Persistent Systems Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 0.89%, ending at Rs.4,776.30, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock’s week was marked by significant volatility, including a sharp gap down on 2 March amid market concerns and a surge in put option activity on 5 March signalling bearish sentiment. These events, combined with technical weakness and sector headwinds, shaped the stock’s cautious but resilient performance. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Emerald Finance Ltd Drops 14.03%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Steep Decline
item.description: Emerald Finance Ltd’s stock endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, plunging 14.03% from Rs.63.13 to Rs.54.27, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% decline over the same period. The stock repeatedly hit fresh 52-week lows amid sustained selling pressure, despite positive quarterly financial results and improved valuation metrics. This review analyses the key events and market dynamics that shaped the stock’s performance during the week. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Balaji Amines Ltd Falls 4.07%: Valuation Shift and 52-Week Low Mark Key Week
item.description: Balaji Amines Ltd’s shares declined by 4.07% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% in the same period. The stock faced significant pressure early in the week, hitting a 52-week low on 2 March, before a modest valuation upgrade on 6 March provided some relief. Despite this, the stock closed the week lower at Rs.1,044.80, reflecting ongoing challenges in the specialty chemicals sector and mixed investor sentiment. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Step Two Corporation Gains 5.00%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Move
item.description: Step Two Corporation Ltd closed the week at Rs.30.45, marking a 5.00% gain from its opening price of Rs.29.00, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. Despite this relative strength, the company was downgraded to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO on 5 March 2026, reflecting emerging technical weaknesses and flat financial trends amid improved valuation metrics. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: MT Educare Ltd Falls 1.36% Despite Multiple Upper Circuit Surges: 4 Key Factors Driving Volatility
item.description: MT Educare Ltd’s stock closed the week at Rs.1.45, down 1.36% from Rs.1.47 the previous Friday, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp intraday rallies hitting upper circuit limits on three separate days, reflecting intense but volatile buying interest amid persistent fundamental challenges and a strong sell rating from analysts. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Nava Ltd Falls 5.03%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Nava Ltd experienced a challenging week on the BSE, closing at Rs.565.60 on 6 March 2026, down 5.03% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.595.55. This decline outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period, reflecting a combination of technical weakness, rating downgrades, and volatile trading sessions that shaped investor sentiment throughout the week. Time: 58 minutes ago

item.title: Epic Energy Ltd Falls 12.61%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Sharp Decline
item.description: Epic Energy Ltd’s shares declined sharply by 12.61% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.29.79 from Rs.34.09 the previous Friday. This underperformance significantly outpaced the Sensex’s 3.00% fall during the same period, reflecting persistent financial challenges, valuation recalibrations, and sectoral headwinds that weighed heavily on the stock’s momentum. Time: 59 minutes ago

item.title: Navkar Urbanstructure Ltd Falls 16.92%: 5 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Navkar Urbanstructure Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its share price declining sharply by 16.92% to close at Rs.1.08, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% fall. The stock oscillated between lower and upper circuit limits amid heavy selling pressure, regulatory freezes, and exceptional volume surges, reflecting a volatile market sentiment for this micro-cap construction firm. Time: 59 minutes ago

item.title: BF Investment Ltd Falls 4.56%: Technical Downgrades and Bearish Momentum Define the Week
item.description: BF Investment Ltd’s shares declined by 4.56% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the BSE Sensex which fell 3.00% in the same period. The stock faced significant downward pressure following a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating amid deteriorating technical indicators and valuation concerns. Despite some short-term positive oscillators, the overall momentum remained bearish, reflecting investor caution amid mixed financial signals. Time: 59 minutes ago
